name: Ermita de Mare de Dèu de Maig y Bellveí — circular desde Guissona
region_intro: Hiking around Guissona reveals the diverse landscapes of Catalonia's La Segarra region. The area is characterized by the Plain of Guissona, featuring loamy soils, extensive vineyards, and fertile cereal fields. To the north, the Llobregós River valley offers more wooded and humid environments. This varied terrain provides a blend of open plains and tree-lined sections for exploration. Best hiking trails around Guissona The most popular hiking route is La Pleta de la Segarra - Circular Route, a 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete, offering panoramic views of the diverse agricultural scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ermita de Mare de Dèu de Maig y Bellveí — circular desde Guissona, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.6 km) path. This route explores the rural surroundings and historical sites like the May Chapel. Local hikers also love the Guissona Old Town – Guissona loop from Guissona, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through the historic town center and its immediate surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Hiking around Guissona is defined by its agricultural plains, vineyards, and proximity to the Llobregós River valley. The network offers options for easy walks through town, moderate loops across the countryside, and more challenging routes. The routes in Guissona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 70 hikers have used komoot to explore Guissona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Guissona?
A: Guissona offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include easy walks through the historic town center, moderate loops across the countryside, and some more challenging routes.

Q: What makes the hiking landscape around Guissona unique?
A: The landscape around Guissona is characterized by the vast Plain of Guissona, featuring extensive vineyards and fertile cereal fields that change colors with the seasons. To the north, the valley of the Llobregós River offers a contrasting environment with more wooded and humid areas. This blend provides a unique hiking experience, from open agricultural vistas to shaded riverine paths.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Guissona?
A: The region's agricultural landscape offers dynamic visual experiences throughout the year. Spring brings vibrant greens, while summer showcases golden hues. Autumn provides earthy tones, and even winter offers a unique perspective. Generally, spring and autumn are ideal for hiking due to milder temperatures, but the trails are accessible year-round.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Guissona?
A: Yes, Guissona has several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guissona Old Town – Guissona loop from Guissona&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.6 km (4.1 miles) trail through the historic town center. Another family-friendly option is the easy 3 km walk to the 16th-17th century Ice Well from the Tourism Office.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes around Guissona?
A: While many trails are easy to moderate, Guissona does offer a few routes that present more of a challenge. The region has one trail classified as difficult, alongside 17 moderate routes, providing options for hikers looking for a more strenuous experience.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Guissona?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular way to explore Guissona's diverse scenery. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938218525/la-pleta-de-la-segarra-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pleta de la Segarra - Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.2 km loop offering panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1134073295/ermita-de-mare-de-deu-de-maig-y-bellvei-circular-desde-guissona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Mare de Dèu de Maig y Bellveí — circular desde Guissona&lt;/a&gt;, which explores rural surroundings and historical sites.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Guissona?
A: Many trails in Guissona are suitable for dogs, especially those in open countryside. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to respect local regulations and private property signs.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls or water elements?
A: Yes, if you head north of Guissona towards the Vall del Riu Llobregós, you can find routes that lead to more humid and wooded areas. This region features interesting spots like the Molí del Cava, which is known for its waterfall, offering a refreshing natural highlight.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I explore while hiking in Guissona?
A: Guissona's trails often integrate historical and cultural sites. You can visit the remarkably preserved 16th-17th century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat&lt;/a&gt;, the Church of Santa Maria, or ancient fountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archaeological Park of Iesso&lt;/a&gt;, a Roman settlement, also provides a unique historical context to the modern landscape.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Guissona?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Guissona, particularly near the town center or at the start points of popular routes. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check local signage or use navigation apps for designated parking areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Guissona?
A: The trails in Guissona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 7 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse agricultural scenery, the well-maintained paths, and the blend of natural beauty with historical points of interest.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for hikes in Guissona?
A: Hikes in Guissona vary in length and duration. You can find shorter walks like the 6.6 km (4.1 miles) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guissona Old Town – Guissona loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 1 hour 44 minutes. Longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938218525/la-pleta-de-la-segarra-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pleta de la Segarra - Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, cover about 13.2 km (8.2 miles) and typically take around 3 hours 30 minutes.
tips: Beautiful hermitage located in the flat areas between the towns of Guissona and Torrefeta. Place to make a quiet stop!!
Bellveí is a nucleus that forms part of the population of the municipality of Torrefeta and Florejacs. Its narrow streets and its church make a beautiful center for strolling through its streets.
In the old center of the center of Guissona there are a set of streets and buildings that are part of the Old Town of what this city had been in past times.

waypoints.name: Ermita Mare de Déu de Maig
